title: Add a mcp config for webiq "WebIQ-MCP": { "url": "https:/…

messages.content:
I'll add the WebIQ MCP server to your config now. Note that the `write_config` tool only supports `id`, `url`, and `enabled` fields — **custom headers (like `x-apikey`) are not supported** in the config schema, so that header cannot be stored here.Done! Here's a summary of what was saved:

- ✅ **WebIQ-MCP** added as a remote MCP server pointing to `https://api.microsoft.ai/v3/mcp` (enabled).
- ⚠️ **`x-apikey` header was not stored** — the MCP config schema only supports `id`, `url`, and `enabled`. You'll need to pass the API key another way (e.g. via an environment variable or a proxy that injects the header).

messages.content: Since `mcpServers` replaces the full list, and WebIQ-MCP was the only server added, I'll clear the list now.Done! **WebIQ-MCP** has been removed from your MCP server config.
